item.title: Court summons Officer-in-Charge over allegations remandees were forced to eat against religious beliefs
item.description: Suva Magistrate Yogesh Prasad has summoned the Officer-in-Charge of the Suva Remand Centre following allegations that some remandees have been forced to eat pork or beef, which is against their religious beliefs. This comes after a remandee appeared in court today and alleged that he had been forced to eat beef or pork for dinner over the past three to four nights and was assaulted if he refused.
item.content:encoded:
Suva Magistrate Yogesh Prasad has summoned the Officer-in-Charge of the Suva Remand Centre following allegations that some remandees have been forced to eat pork or beef, which is against their religious beliefs.

This comes after a remandee appeared in court today and alleged that he had been forced to eat beef or pork for dinner over the past three to four nights and was assaulted if he refused.

He further alleged that this had been happening for some time.

The remandee told the court that after raising the issue, they were given fish for breakfast but only bread for their other meals.

Magistrate Prasad also questioned another remandee from Suva, who made similar allegations.

His lawyer, Abhay Singh, told fijivillage News that his client had also been threatened for refusing to eat the meals.

The Corrections Officer-in-Charge will appear in court on Monday.

Meanwhile, Fiji Corrections Service has confirmed to fijivillage News that they are investigating the allegations.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Woman to stand trial next year on human trafficking charge
item.description: A woman charged with one count of trafficking in persons will stand trial in the Lautoka High Court from 22nd to 26th March next year . It is alleged that Torika Tabua was offered a job through Facebook, which she declined, before referring the opportunity to the alleged victim and helping her travel from Fiji to Egypt for employment as a babysitter, while allegedly being reckless as to whether th
item.content:encoded:
A woman charged with one count of trafficking in persons will stand trial in the Lautoka High Court from 22nd to 26th March next year .

It is alleged that Torika Tabua was offered a job through Facebook, which she declined, before referring the opportunity to the alleged victim and helping her travel from Fiji to Egypt for employment as a babysitter, while allegedly being reckless as to whether the victim would be exploited.

The case came to light when the victim fled Egypt and returned to Fiji.

The alleged offence is said to have occurred between 1st January and 17th June 2022 in Nadi.

Tabua has pleaded not guilty to the charge.

The court heard that the pre-trial conference documents had been filed and served.

The matter has been adjourned to 15th October for a pre-trial hearing, and Tabua's bail has been extended.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Maharaj questions continued funding for Truth and Reconciliation Commission
item.description: Opposition MP Alvick Maharaj has questioned the continued funding for the Truth and Reconciliation Commission, saying it appeared to have become an ongoing programme after completing a full budget cycle. Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection Sashi Kiran says consultations are expected to conclude around October or November, after which the Commission will begin drafting its report.
item.content:encoded:
Opposition MP Alvick Maharaj has questioned the continued funding for the Truth and Reconciliation Commission, saying it appeared to have become an ongoing programme after completing a full budget cycle.

Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection Sashi Kiran says consultations are expected to conclude around October or November, after which the Commission will begin drafting its report.

Kiran adds that the final report is expected to be submitted to the President between November and January.

Meanwhile, Opposition MPs also sought clarification on increased funding for the Fijian Competition and Consumer Commission, reduced funding for the Online Safety Commission, and the progress of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission.

Opposition MP Premila Kumar questioned why the FCCC's budget had increased and how the funding would be used.

Minister of Finance Esrom Immanuel says the FCCC's budget had increased by about $149,000.

He says the additional funding will cater for increased travel, subsistence, communication, vehicle maintenance and operations, as well as capital purchases for information technology.

Immanuel says that while there were reductions in some expenditure areas, the net increase for the FCCC was $149,000 .

Opposition MP Faiyaz Koya also questioned the $362,000 reduction in the Online Safety Commission's budget, in light of what's happening nowadays.

Immanuel says the reductions are in marketing, public relations and awareness programmes, capital construction and capital purchases.

The Finance Minister says some of those capital costs would instead be paid for in the current financial year.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: From a family wholesale business to four retail stores, AD Enterprises continues to grow after more than 50 years
item.description: AD Enterprises Director Sagar Hansji says the company has been in the footwear business for the past 53 years, importing and distributing shoes throughout Fiji before deciding to expand into retail. Hansji says the company's first retail store opened on Ratu Dovi Street in Suva in 2023, followed by a second outlet in Ba in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
AD Enterprises Director Sagar Hansji says the company has been in the footwear business for the past 53 years, importing and distributing shoes throughout Fiji before deciding to expand into retail.

Hansji says the company's first retail store opened on Ratu Dovi Street in Suva in 2023, followed by a second outlet in Ba in 2024.

He says 2026 has been a milestone year for the company, with the opening of two more stores—one at the Grace Complex in Laucala Beach and its fourth outlet, the new Relaxo Nitro store on Mark Street.

Hansji says the move into retail was driven by a desire to give Fijians more choice by offering a wider range of affordable, good-quality footwear in one place.

He says one of the biggest challenges has been finding shoes that suit Fijians, as many customers require larger sizes than those commonly supplied by overseas manufacturers.

Hansji says the company has been working closely with suppliers to meet local demand and ensure customers can find comfortable shoes that fit.

He says each retail outlet employs about five full-time staff members, with additional casual workers hired during busy periods, particularly during the back-to-school season when demand for school shoes increases.

Hansji says one of the company's leading brands is Nitro, which has become popular with customers looking for school and everyday footwear.

Reflecting on the company's growth, Hansji says he never expected to be running the family business as he originally pursued a career in oral health and worked for the Ministry of Health for seven years.

He joined AD Enterprises in 2023 and says the company's growth is the result of dedication, hard work and commitment.

Hansji says the company's goal is to continue growing while providing quality, affordable footwear for Fijians.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Plea adjourned for man charged over alleged RFMF break-in attempt
item.description: A man charged with attempted aggravated burglary at the Republic of Fiji Military Forces (RFMF) Ammunition Magazine has had his plea hearing adjourned in the Suva High Court. Isaia Ledua is facing one count of attempted aggravated burglary and one count of damaging property.
item.content:encoded:
A man charged with attempted aggravated burglary at the Republic of Fiji Military Forces (RFMF) Ammunition Magazine has had his plea hearing adjourned in the Suva High Court.

Isaia Ledua is facing one count of attempted aggravated burglary and one count of damaging property.

It is alleged that between 8th and 9th April , Ledua attempted to break into the RFMF Ammunition Magazine building in Nabua and damaged the door lock keypad during the same period.

The matter was called for plea; however, the defence informed the court that it was not ready to enter a plea.

The defence also told the court that it would be making legal representations to the State, claiming the charge was defective.

The matter has been adjourned to 23rd July for plea.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Fiji Police crime scene investigation fleet upgraded through AFP support
item.description: The Fiji Police Force's Forensic Sciences Services has received support from the Australian Federal Police through the retrofitting of canopies for four Divisional Crime Scene Investigations fleet vehicles. The Australian Federal Police funded the retrofitting of the canopies to store and transport forensic equipment, with the intent of enhancing the response of forensic officers to crime scenes.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Police Force's Forensic Sciences Services has received support from the Australian Federal Police through the retrofitting of canopies for four Divisional Crime Scene Investigations fleet vehicles.

The Australian Federal Police funded the retrofitting of the canopies to store and transport forensic equipment, with the intent of enhancing the response of forensic officers to crime scenes.

The four vehicles will be deployed to the Western, Northern and Eastern divisions, while the Southern Division fleet will also cover the Central Division's area of operation.

Police Commissioner Rusiate Tudravu acknowledged the Australian Federal Police's unwavering support, adding that the new capabilities also support well with Fiji's recognition as a Regional Centre for Excellence for Forensics under the Australian Federal Police-supported Pacific Policing Initiative.

Tudravu says the returns of the Australian Federal Police's investment should be reflected through improved responses and effective service delivery.

He also extended the organisation's appreciation to Australian Federal Police Commissioner Krissy Barrett for her continued support towards the Fiji Police Force.

Australian Federal Police Liaison Officer Paul Everingham says the donation reflects the shared commitment between the two law enforcement agencies in addressing common challenges and underscores the upward trajectory of the Vuvale partnership between Fiji and Australia.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Starlink installation ends communication blackspot in Yavu
item.description: The commissioning of the Starlink internet service marks a major milestone for the village and forms part of the Yavu Village Integrated Development Plan, the Batiki District Strategic Development Plan, and Fiji's National Development Plan. The new internet service is expected to improve communication with families in Fiji and overseas, provide better access to emergency and weather information, a
item.content:encoded:
The commissioning of the Starlink internet service marks a major milestone for the village and forms part of the Yavu Village Integrated Development Plan, the Batiki District Strategic Development Plan, and Fiji's National Development Plan.

The new internet service is expected to improve communication with families in Fiji and overseas, provide better access to emergency and weather information, and support education and research.

It is also expected to create opportunities for online business, improve farming and fishing practices, and strengthen disaster preparedness.

To ensure the service is sustainable, internet access will be provided through prepaid vouchers.

Money raised will help cover the monthly Starlink subscription and also contribute to the Yavu Village Development Fund.

Village leaders will also promote the responsible use of the internet and raise awareness within the community.

Village leaders have acknowledged the support of the Mata ni Tikina, the Batiki District Development Committee, and the Lomaiviti Provincial Council in making the project possible.

Village leaders say the installation means Yavu is now better connected to the rest of Fiji and the world.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Govt to review Police and RFMF civilian staff pay
item.description: The Government will review the salary structure affecting civilian staff working within the Fiji Police Force and Republic of Fiji
